name: Soft Molasses Cookies
directions: Preheat oven to 400F.
Grease cookie sheets.
In large bowl with mixer at medium speed, beat all ingredients except raisins until well blended.
Drop mixture by rounded teaspoonfuls, at least 2 inches apart, onto cookie sheets.
Place 3 to 5 raisins on top of each cookie, if desired.
Bake 8 minutes or until top of cookie springs back when lightly pressed with finger.
Remove cookies from cookie sheet and place on wire rack to cool.

name: Molasses Cookies
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Cream sugar and shortening.
Add egg.
Add molasses.
Beat well then blend in sifted dry ingredients.
Drop by teaspoon onto greased cookie sheet.
Bake 10 minutes or less- watch cookies as molasses can burn easily.

name: Soft Molasses Cookies
directions: Mix molasses, sugar, and shortening.  Add spices salt and baking soda dissolved in 1 tbsp cold water.
Add eggs, raisins and milk.  Mix dry ingredients and stir in .
Drop batter by teaspoons on greased baking sheet.
Bake 10-15 minutes.

name: Grandma May's Molasses Cookies
directions: Mix cooled shortening, sugar, molasses, water and egg together well.
Blend in dry ingredients.
Chill a short time in refrigerator.
Make 1-inch balls and roll in granulated sugar ( I usually add a little bit of cinnamon to the sugar).
Bake on greased cookie sheets at 375° for 12-15 minutes.

name: Frosted Molasses Cookies
directions: Cream shortening and brown sugar.
Stir in eggs and molasses.
Stir together the flour and spices in a separate bowl.
Add the milk alternately with flour mixture until blended, but don't overmix.
Drop by tablespoonfuls onto greased baking sheet.
Bake at 350 degrees 10-12 minutes.
Cool and frost with buttercream frosting.

name: Molasses Cookies
directions: Preheat oven to 375.
Cream butter, sugar, molasses, and egg.
Mix remaining dry ingredients.
Combine wet and dry ingredients.
Add chocolate chips or raisins.
Drop by teaspoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake for 8-10 minutes.

name: Chocolate Chip Molasses Cookies
directions: In a large mixing bowl, beat the shortening, sugar and molasses.  Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Combine the flour, baking soda and salt;gradually add to creamed mixture.  Stir in chocolate chips.
Roll into 1-in. balls.  Place 2 inches apart on ungreased baking sheets.  Bake at 375 for 8-10 minutes or until edges are firm.  Removed to wire racks.

name: Crisp & Chewy Molasses Cookies
directions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, cloves, ginger, and sugar.  Add the soft butter, molasses and egg beating until smooth (about 2 minutes).
Lightly stir in the rolled oats, just until mixed.  Use a level tablespoon of the dough for each cookie.  Drop the dough 2 inches apart on ungreased baking sheets.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, do not over bake.  If you like chewy cookies, remove them from the oven earlier (they will be lighter colour in the middle).
Allow the cookies to stay on the cookie sheets a minute or so to get firm before removing then to a cooling rack.
When cool, store in an air tight container.

name: Grandmas Chocolate  Molasses  Cookies
directions: cream shortening  and sugar.
add eggs.
add molasses
sift dry ingredients
add to creamed mixture  alternately  with milk.
mix until  smooth
drop by teaspoon  onto greased  baking  sheets  bake 350 degrees no time given 10 minutes  molasses  burns check
or add nuts and raisins.
you can make peanut butter just decrease  shortening to 1/4 add 1/2 c peanut butter.
